doc.description:
Per-station, per-(year, month) rollup produced by
AnalyzeStationMonthly. Nullable numeric fields encode "no data
for the month" so downstream consumers can distinguish absence
from a genuine zero.

Filesystem paths of the on-disk bundle produced by
GenerateClimateReport. Lives under
``$AFL_CACHE_ROOT/noaa-weather/climate-report/<country>/<region>/``.

Discover GHCN-Daily stations by country, state, and/or Geofabrik region.
Reads ghcnd-stations.txt and ghcnd-inventory.txt to verify data availability
before returning stations. Filters by min_years of coverage and required elements.

``region`` accepts any Geofabrik path — ``europe/germany``,
``north-america/us/california``. When set, the region's bounding box
becomes the spatial filter and overrides ``country`` unless ``country``
is passed explicitly (non-default).

Download the GHCN-Daily CSV for a station from S3.
One file per station contains ALL years — no per-year loop needed.
Filters records to [start_year, end_year] range during download.

Analyze a station across its full date range in one handler call.
Reads the downloaded CSV, computes daily/yearly aggregates (TMAX, TMIN, PRCP),
and produces per-year climate summaries.

Compute region-level climate trend from station analyses.
Aggregates all station reports for a country+state, runs linear regression
on temperature and precipitation, generates narrative summary.
  this step waits for all station analyses to complete before aggregating.

params.name: station_count
params.description: Dependency signal — pass discovery.station_count to ensure

doc.description:
Produce per-(year, month) rollups for a single station.
Reads the station's cached GHCN CSV, computes monthly climate
aggregates (mean/min/max temp, total precip, hot/frost/precip days).
Output shape matches the MonthlyClimate schema, returned as JSON
so FFL callers can forage without per-month schema overhead.
Reverse geocode station coordinates via OSM Nominatim.

Generate a regional climate report bundle: JSON aggregates,
Markdown narrative, self-contained HTML, and five SVG charts
(climograph, annual_trend, warming_stripes, heatmap, anomaly_bars).

Region selection accepts ``region`` (Geofabrik path) OR
``country``+``state`` — matches the DiscoverStations vocabulary.
``baseline_start`` / ``baseline_end`` define the climate-normals
window (default 1991–2020, the current WMO standard).

Returns filesystem paths to every file in the bundle.

List every Geofabrik region under a path prefix.

Used by GenerateRegionGroupReport's ``andThen foreach`` to fan
out a single "report on every province" workflow. Mirrors the
``climate-report.sh --all-under PREFIX --include-parents --list``
CLI mode.

``prefix = ""`` returns every region in the cached Geofabrik
index — usually a footgun (tens of thousands of regions).
``include_parents = false`` (default) returns leaves only;
``true`` also returns the prefix + every intermediate level.
Analyze a single GHCN-Daily station: fetch CSV, analyze climate, reverse geocode.

Analyze climate trends for a state: discover stations, analyze each, compute trend.
DiscoverStations -> foreach station -> (FetchStationData, AnalyzeStationClimate, ReverseGeocode) -> ComputeRegionTrend.
Analyze climate trends for all 50 US states in parallel.

End-to-end regional climate report: discover → fetch → monthly rollups
→ render bundle (JSON + Markdown + HTML + 5 SVGs).

This workflow maps 1:1 onto the `climate-report.sh` CLI — the
handler for GenerateClimateReport re-runs discovery + download
internally so the report is always self-contained.
Use `DiscoverStations` + `AnalyzeStationMonthly` ahead of time
if you want to warm the cache first; the report handler will
read the cached CSVs rather than re-downloading.

Same as GenerateRegionReport but filters stations by a Geofabrik
region path (``europe/germany``, ``north-america/us/california``).
The region's bounding box is the spatial filter.

Report on every Geofabrik region under a prefix — e.g. Canada
plus every province, Europe plus every country, or any sub-tree
of the Geofabrik hierarchy.

Equivalent to::

    ./climate-report.sh --all-under <prefix> [--include-parents]

Fans out via ``ListRegionsUnder`` + ``andThen foreach`` so every
report runs as its own step with its own logs, retries, and
output bundle. Individual failures are caught and absorbed so
one bad region doesn't poison the batch.

``include_parents = true`` also runs the prefix region itself
(for "Canada as a whole plus each province") — leaves only by
default.
Analyze climate trends for all 13 Canadian provinces/territories.
Analyze climate trends for Russia.
Analyze climate trends for India.
Analyze climate trends for Mexico.
Analyze climate trends for Antarctica research stations.
Analyze climate trends for 10 South American countries.
Analyze climate trends for 18 European countries.
Analyze climate trends for 13 African countries.
Analyze climate trends for 7 Asian countries.
Analyze climate trends for 3 Arctic territories.

Cache GHCN-Daily data for one state: discover stations, download each station's CSV.
One FetchStationData per station (all years in one CSV).
Cache GHCN-Daily data for all 50 US states.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for all 13 Canadian provinces/territories.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for Russia.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for India.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for Mexico.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for Antarctica research stations.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for 10 South American countries.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for 18 European countries.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for 13 African countries.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for 7 Asian countries.
Cache GHCN-Daily data for 3 Arctic territories.
